infrastructure.name: Kwinana Freeway Widening (Russell–Mortimer Rd)
infrastructure.notes: $700M upgrade, 3rd lane each way over 11km. Construction 2027.

noise.notes: Minor Kwinana Fwy noise eastern streets

forward.consensus: Strong mid-cycle with infrastructure tailwinds. Base case 30-35% total 5yr growth.

suburbs.name: Wellard

infrastructure.name: Wellard Train Station (existing)
infrastructure.notes: On Mandurah Line, 42min to Perth CBD. Major value driver.

publicHousing.details: Mostly private estates, small social housing footprint

rentalDemand.tenantTypes: First home buyer renters, young families, Kwinana industrial workers

noise.notes: Kwinana industrial area 8km north — occasional industrial noise. Fwy proximity.

forward.consensus: Solid mid-tier pick with train-station premium. Base case 30% total 5yr growth.

suburbs.stampDutyNote: At $710k: stamp ~$23k, total ~$85k — very comfortable fit

suburbs.name: Armadale

infrastructure.name: Metronet Byford Rail Extension (Armadale Line)
infrastructure.notes: COMPLETED Oct 2025. New elevated Armadale station, 9 level crossings removed, 8km new dual track to Byford. Major transformation — expect continued price uplift.

notableTypes: property crime above state avg but declining

publicHousing.details: Some public housing in southern Armadale streets. Concentration declining.

forward.consensus: Strong early-cycle with Metronet catalyst just activating. Highest growth upside in Perth mid-ring. Base case 35-40% 5yr.

suburbs.stampDutyNote: At $600k: stamp ~$18k, total ~$72k — huge cash buffer remains

suburbs.name: Mandurah

infrastructure.name: Mandurah Train Line (existing)
infrastructure.notes: 70min to Perth CBD. Transperth Mandurah Line terminus.

infrastructure.name: Peel Business Park
infrastructure.notes: Major employment precinct creating local jobs.

flood.zone: Estuary flood zones in coastal streets

connectivity.publicTransport: Mandurah train (terminus of line) + local bus

risks: Coastal flood zones in some streets
forward.consensus: High-growth regional-metro play. Strong momentum but watch for moderation. Base case 30% 5yr.

watchPoints: Estuary flood zones — check specific street

suburbs.name: Gawler

infrastructure.name: Northern Adelaide Urban Growth (43,300 new homes Two Wells/Roseworthy)
infrastructure.notes: Urban growth boundary removed. 43,300 dwellings planned. Massive population inflow expected.

infrastructure.name: Gawler Line Electrification
infrastructure.notes: Already delivered — electric trains, faster commute to Adelaide CBD (~50min).

publicHousing.details: Some older public housing but not dominant

forward.consensus: Strong early-cycle play with massive infrastructure tailwind. Watch oversupply risk late-cycle. Base 28-32% 5yr.

suburbs.stampDutyNote: At $637k: stamp ~$24k, total ~$95k — comfortable fit within $110k cash

suburbs.name: Salisbury Downs

infrastructure.name: Gawler Line Electrification (Salisbury station)
infrastructure.notes: Electric trains directly serve Salisbury station. Commute improvement.

infrastructure.name: Edinburgh Parks Defence/Industrial Precinct
infrastructure.notes: Major defence industry precinct driving employment in northern Adelaide.

notableTypes: property crime well above state avg
safer than only 4% of AU suburbs

publicHousing.details: City of Salisbury historically higher public housing concentration

forward.consensus: Growth exists but crime + IRSAD concerns cap upside. Better Adelaide alternatives available. Base case 20-25% 5yr.

suburbs.stampDutyNote: At $680k: stamp ~$26k, total ~$97k — fits but risks outweigh savings. Consider Gawler instead.

suburbs.name: Geraldton

infrastructure.name: Oakajee Strategic Industrial Area (green hydrogen)
infrastructure.notes: Major green hydrogen + renewables precinct. Long-term catalyst.

noise.notes: Port and industrial areas on western edge

forward.consensus: High-growth regional play with excellent fundamentals. Regional risk is main concern. Base case 25-35% 5yr.

suburbs.stampDutyNote: At $473k: stamp ~$14k, total ~$58k — massive cash buffer. Can afford building inspection risk.

suburbs.name: Albany

infrastructure.name: Albany Health Campus expansion
infrastructure.notes: Regional healthcare hub expansion creates local jobs.

forward.consensus: Diversified regional with lifestyle appeal. More resilient than pure mining towns. Base case 22-28% 5yr.

suburbs.stampDutyNote: At $550k: stamp ~$20k, total ~$75k — excellent cash buffer

suburbs.name: Melton

infrastructure.name: Melton Rail Electrification
infrastructure.notes: Long-awaited upgrade. Currently diesel. Timeline uncertain — federal funding dependent.

infrastructure.name: Western Outer Metropolitan Freeway
infrastructure.notes: Major freeway connecting outer west. Early planning.

rentalDemand.tenantTypes: Young families, first home buyer renters

risks: VIC STAMP DUTY ~$21k on $510k = $85k total costs (tight but fits)
forward.consensus: Affordable entry but Melbourne underperforming. VIC stamp duty bite. Base case 15-22% 5yr — well below Perth/Adelaide.

suburbs.stampDutyNote: At $510k: VIC stamp ~$21k, total costs ~$83k — fits $110k. BUT growth ceiling lower than WA alternatives.

suburbs.name: Mount Gambier

forward.consensus: Affordable regional but limited upside. Better regional options exist (Geraldton/Albany WA). Base case 12-18% 5yr.

suburbs.stampDutyNote: At $530k: stamp ~$20k, total ~$80k — affordable. But growth upside limited vs WA regional.

suburbs.name: Ballarat Central

infrastructure.name: GovHub Ballarat
infrastructure.notes: Government employment hub driving CBD jobs.

forward.consensus: Stable but slow. VIC stamp duty kills investor returns. Much better WA/SA alternatives. Base case 10-15% 5yr.

suburbs.stampDutyNote: At $550k: VIC stamp ~$22k, total ~$88k — fits but growth upside poor. Pass in favour of WA regional.

summary: 11 suburbs analyzed across 7 regions. Top BUY picks: Armadale WA (Metronet catalyst, $600k, score 81), Mandurah WA (momentum +23.7%, $575k, score 85), Geraldton WA (highest growth, $473k, score 84), Baldivis WA (master-planned, $750k, score 82), Albany WA (diversified regional, $550k, score 83), Wellard WA (train station, $710k, score 81), Gawler SA (43k homes pipeline, $637k, score 79). WATCH: Salisbury Downs (high crime flag), Melton VIC (affordable but VIC stamp duty). CAUTION: Mount Gambier (regional liquidity), Ballarat (slow growth). For $800k budget targeting growth + WA/SA diversification: Armadale or Mandurah offer best combination of price, growth upside, and infrastructure catalysts. Gawler is the standout SA option. Avoid Davoren Park/Elizabeth area (high crime, low IRSAD).
